root_text.an6.89:1.1: “Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo diṭṭhisampadaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.89:1.3: Sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ, vicikicchaṁ, sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ, apāyagamanīyaṁ rāgaṁ, apāyagamanīyaṁ dosaṁ, apāyagamanīyaṁ mohaṁ.
root_text.an6.89:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme appahāya abhabbo diṭṭhisampadaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.89:2.1: Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo diṭṭhisampadaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.89:2.3: Sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ, vicikicchaṁ, sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ, apāyagamanīyaṁ rāgaṁ, apāyagamanīyaṁ dosaṁ, apāyagamanīyaṁ mohaṁ.
root_text.an6.89:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme pahāya bhabbo diṭṭhisampadaṁ sacchikātun”ti.

translation_text.an6.89:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up six things you can’t become accomplished in view.
translation_text.an6.89:1.3: Substantialist view, doubt, misapprehension of precepts and observances, and forms of greed, hate, and delusion that lead to rebirth in places of loss.
translation_text.an6.89:1.4: Without giving up these six things you can’t become accomplished in view.
translation_text.an6.89:2.1: After giving up six things you can become accomplished in view.
translation_text.an6.89:2.3: Substantialist view, doubt, misapprehension of precepts and observances, and forms of greed, hate, and delusion that lead to rebirth in places of loss.
translation_text.an6.89:2.4: After giving up these six things you can become accomplished in view.”
